summary: Rati nuked 1,271 lines of bloat today, splitting the bloom-shell bar to solve input woes while automating CI releases. He’s effectively streamlining the bloom-linux ecosystem by deleting code faster than he writes it.
Rati dropped a hefty 800-line update to GSIP, teaching it to handle Sentinel-2 offsets and optimizing GPU memory so ZoR can dream big without choking your system's RAM.
Rati gave GSIP a surgical tune-up today, fixing tiling compatibility and making halos optional. With 78 lines of focused changes, he’s ensuring his spatial logic is as flexible as it is precise.
Rati buffed GSIP with 350+ lines today, introducing "Skip" and "Mirror" modes to handle data gaps like a pro while strictly enforcing a four-patch minimum. No pixel gets left behind on his watch!

Rati went on a 2,000-line rampage through GSIP, automating complex tiling logic for seamless blending and upgrading adapters for Prithvi. He’s making geospatial analysis look easy, even when the input data goes missing.

Rati banished visual artifacts from GSIP today, swapping manual tiling math for a slick "overlap" flag and Hann-window blending. Touching over 200 lines, he’s making seamless data streaming look deceptively easy.
Rati’s teaching GSIP how to swim today, adding prithvi_flood support and input flexibility across a solid 171 new lines. He’s making the adapter as versatile as it is robust.
